title: Examples, Delimiters, Templates, and Structured Output · Failure Triage
summary: Stabilize a classification and extraction prompt across varied inputs. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a tested prompt template with diverse examples and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose relevant and diverse examples that cover boundaries without teaching accidental shortcuts.
Include counterexamples and near misses when category boundaries are material.
Separate instructions, context, examples, and variable data with clear headings or provider-appropriate tags.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-template-versioning-dnrels" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Template Versioning into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-template-versioning-dnrels
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Template Versioning applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Version fixed prompt text separately from variable inputs and evaluation data.
